observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from granting incentives without any legal basis pursuant to R.A. No. 6758 and COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Management had appealed the disallowance.
observations.reason: Under appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all employees concerned to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 and to henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management appealed the disallowance.
observations.reason: Under appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the PRAISE Committee, in coordination with the City Budget Officer, to determine the monetary savings as a result of the exemplary efforts and superior accomplishments of officials and employees pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01 series of 2001. Monetary awards shall be granted only to individuals or group of individuals upon determining that their suggestions, inventions, and superior accomplishments resulted in monetary savings for the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented – Monetary savings determination would be submitted later subject to availability of records.
observations.reason: Monetary savings determination would be submitted later subject to availability of records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the PPE Inventory Committee:
a) hasten its procedures in the conduct of physical count of inventories,
and submit the required reports such as the RCPPE, ICF, List of PPEs
found at Station and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs; and
b) follow the appropriate numbering system, pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We also recommended that the Office of the City Accountant collaborate
with the PPE Inventory Committee to ensure that all PPEs included in the RCPPE
are duly reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommended that the City Government demand the immediate
payment of the remaining balance of the financial assistance granted to
BACEMCO.

We further recommended that Management desist from granting
additional loans to private cooperatives which are prohibited under Sections 305
(b) and 335 of R.A No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Accountant implement the imprest system
in the establishment and maintenance of the Petty Cash Fund (PCF). We also
recommended that Management review and evaluate the sufficiency of the
approved amount of ₱300,000.00 for petty cash expenses and ensure that it is
followed accordingly.

We recommended that the City Accountant should ensure that the grant
of cash advances for the PCF is supported with the required estimate of expenses
and that it is not used for regular and recurring expenses or those which can be
drawn from regular or special purpose cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE refrain from drawing cash advances for
confidential purposes unless the previous cash advances are liquidated pursuant
to Sections 6.1.5, 6.2.2 and 6.2.2.1 of COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission
for GOCCs and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015. We
further recommended that liquidation documents be submitted to COA-ICFAU
through other means allowed under the Joint Circular, which are through
registered mail or courier, to settle the advance.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee require the
personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the City Mayor, as Head
of the Procuring Entity, to fill up the relevant and required information in the PO
pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. We also
recommended that the BAC should refrain from awarding a contract unless the
RFQ contains the required details of the goods procured.

We recommended that Management appropriate ₱569,750.00 in the
General Fund and transfer the same to the BGCM as reimbursement for the cost
of the procurement of communication equipment, computer laptops and office
supplies. We further recommended that Management ensure to use its remaining
BGCM in accordance with DBM Budget Circular No. 125 dated, April 7, 2020.

We recommended that the LDRRMO, henceforth, limit the utilization of
the LDRRM Fund to activities related only to disaster preparedness, prevention
and mitigation measures such as risk assessment, capability building, purchase of
early warning systems and preparedness equipment, among others, in order to
boost its ability to respond to or mitigate the effects of calamities which might
occur.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize
the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities:
(a) conduct risk assessments considering new variables after the pandemic; (b)
identify critical processes and functions; (c) determine scenarios that may disrupt
normal operations; (d) conduct risk and impact analyses; € formulate the PSCP;
and (f) submit the plan to the CDRRMO for review, to the LCE for approval, and
to the Sangguniang Panglungsod for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize
the PSCP.

We further recommended and Management agreed to take proactive
measures in being informed and updated of relevant issuances by authorities that
may affect their local governance performance scorecards such as the DILG’s Seal
of Good Local Governance and comply with provisions therein, like the
submission of PSCP.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of transferring 20%
Development fund appropriations to Barangays for the latter’s implementation
and instead address the City Engineering Office’s inadequacy in coping with the
number of projects expected to be implemented annually.

We further recommended that Management appropriate ₱1,200,000.00 in
the General Fund Proper and transfer the same to the 20% DF as reimbursement
for the funds transferred to barangays in order that the minimum amount of
appropriations for the 20% Development Fund as required under Section 287 of
the Local Government Code may be complied with.

We recommended that Management require the Medical Scholarship
Board (MSB) to:
a) include the financial capacity of the applicant’s family as one of the
eligibility criteria for the “Our-Bario Doctor-Our Scholar” medical
scholarship program;
b) identify and implement stringent sanctions for the non-compliance of
the mandatory return of service to ensure the integration of scholars
into the public health and medical service system of the LGU, especially
in barangays;
c) establish a specific set of criteria in the selection and accreditation of
medical schools included in the program to ensure an exceptional
education for the City’s scholars;
d) add a stipulation in the IRR regarding reimbursements for
downpayments made by qualified scholars; and
e) improve information dissemination in the LGU and each barangay to
increase the number of applicants and scholars of the program.

We further recommended that the MSB re-evaluate the IRR and strictly
implement procedures and sanctions provided in case of failure to comply
conditions of the scholarship.

We recommended that Management and the BAC strictly implement the
qualification and approval process in procurement, according to the provisions of
the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184, to ensure integrity of procedures.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the HoPE impose
administrative penalties to erring bidders, pursuant to Section 69 of the same IRR.

We recommended and Management agreed, in coordination with the
CSWD, to establish the following remedial measures to regulate the grant of
financial assistance:
a) Create policies and guidelines on the grant of financial
assistance/donations in conformity with DSWD MC No. 11, s. 2019, as
amended;
b) Always refer cases to the CSWDO for eligibility evaluation and case
study report; and
c) Henceforth, ensure that all disbursement for financial assistance be
supported with the appropriate and complete requirements, pursuant
to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We further recommended and Management also agreed to submit the
abovementioned lacking documents or an explanation/justification of the grant of
medical and burial assistance sans the documentary requirements.

We recommended that Management stop spending on advertisements of
its community development information outside of Bayawan City to avoid the
incurrence of unnecessary expenditures, pursuant to COA Circular No. 85-55-A
dated September 8, 1985, as updated by COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated
October 29, 2012.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person strictly adhere to Item V of
COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, for the timely submission of
the said reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) establish an M&E System through the issuance of an Executive Order
or Administrative Order;
b) oblige the M&E Team, through its GFPS, to conduct an assessment and
evaluation of all the gender-responsive or GAD-focused policies,
programs, projects, accomplishments, and actual costs for the past
three years during his administration;
c) task them to provide recommendations based on its assessment and
evaluation, when necessary; and
d) ensure that the M&E Team consolidate the results of the assessment
and evaluation in a “GAD Evaluation Report,” which shall be
submitted to the DILG Regional Office no later than June of the next
term.

We recommended that the GFPS prepare and set up the GAD Agenda of
the City Government and identify priority gender issues of the LGU, for the next
three-year term of leadership, pursuant to Section 4, Item C.2 (1) of the PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1, as amended.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to make an adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables as of December 31, 2020.
We further recommended that she record only the total Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables per barangay at the beginning of the year in the eNGAS, instead of all the RPUs, to avoid failure in the system.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had already made the adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management demand liquidation of the transferred funds and record the unliquidated balances as receivables in the books.
We further recommended that, henceforth, Management enter into a MOA with the City Schools Division of the participating schools to the Tawo-Tawo Festival before transferring funds intended to defray the expenses in connection therewith.
observations.managementAction: The transferred funds had already been liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management appropriate ₱2,550,000.00 in the General Fund and reimburse the BGCM Special Account for the cost of the Isuzu Reefer Van and henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 on the use of the remaining funds.
observations.managementAction: Management had already reimbursed the amount from the General Fund Proper.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 on the utilization of the BGCM.
We further recommended that, henceforth, the BAC conduct the procurement of motor vehicles in accordance with Section 10 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184 to ensure transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management already adhered with the proper utilization of the BGCM. Also, motorvehicles were already procured in accordance with the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that procurement of welfare goods for distribution to the constituents is in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.
We also recommended, that, henceforth, Management ensure that the remaining funds of the BGCM shall be used in accordance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 in order to mitigate and contain the transmission of COVID-19.
observations.managementAction: Management procured the welfare goods for distribution in accordance with the GPPB Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed, to, henceforth, ensure that the remaining funds of the BGCM be used in accordance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 in order to mitigate and contain the transmission of COVID-19.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer used the BGCM funds for material assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management submitted a written explanation on the deficiencies noted. We also recommended that the BAC, henceforth, comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure the integrity and transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.
observations.managementAction: For the year audited, BAC already ensured that requirements are complied with before bidding and entering into a contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC, henceforth, ensure that all the procedures in the procurement process are judiciously performed and that all projects, especially infrastructure projects, are meticulously planned and included in the APP before they are undertaken.
observations.managementAction: BAC already incorporated all LGU projects in their Annual Procurement Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the BAC refrain from bidding design and build projects unless the required preliminary design and construction studies have been sufficiently carried out and duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity in accordance with Item 7, Annex G of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: For the current year, BAC already ensured the requirements are complied with before bidding and entering into a contract. Management explained that when the JVA was entered into in CY 2020, a technical team was responsible for handling all the technical aspect, including the preliminary designs and construction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management already submitted a written explanation on the deficiencies noted. We also recommended that, henceforth, the BAC comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure the integrity and transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.
observations.managementAction: BAC already ensured that all projects opened for bidding have approved appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the BAC agreed to, henceforth, ensure that all the procedures in the procurement process are judiciously performed and that all projects, especially infrastructure projects, are meticulously planned and included in the APP before they are undertaken.
observations.managementAction: FMR and other projects for implementation in the audited year were already incorporated in the Annual Procurement Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds without appropriation in accordance with Section 305 (a) of R.A No. 7160 and Section 85(1) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that said projects were funded proceeds. However, all disbursements in CY 2021 were supported with appropriation ordinances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and BAC submitted an explanation why the bid of Legacy Construction Corporation was not marked “failed” despite the lacking documents during the opening of bids. We further recommended that, henceforth, the BAC conduct the City’s procurement of infrastructure projects in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: For the year audited, BAC already ensured the submission of the SEC Registration Certificate, Business Permit, and Tax Clearance of bidders during public bidding. Those bidders without the aforementioned requirements were marked as “failed.”

observations.recommendation: We recommended that meals and snacks served during regular and administrative meetings of local officials and employees be charged to the appropriations for EME and representation allowances of officials concerned. We further recommended that Management refrain from conducting regular and administrative meetings outside the City Hall to avoid additional and unnecessary expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management already charged the expenses for meals and snacks to the appropriate funds and refrained from conducting regular and administrative meetings outside the City Hall, unless necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC post in the PhilGEPS all NOAs and NTPs in accordance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: BAC Office already coordinated with the City Engineering Office and observed timely posting of NOAs and NTPs, in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and the TWG comply strictly with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the conduct of eligibility check and post-qualification procedures to ensure that government contracts are awarded to qualified contractors.
observations.managementAction: BAC and TWG are now carefully examining eligibility documents of the contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government immediately enter into a MOA with PCSO pursuant to Section 40 of the 2016 Revised IRR for the STL Operations of the PCSO so that the STL share can be utilized for health programs, medical assistance and services, and charities of the City.
observations.managementAction: Management already entered into a MOA with PCSO last March 23, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant and CGSO agreed to reconcile their records to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of the PPE accounts in the financial statements pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management already reconciled accounting records per eNGAS and the GSO based on Inventory System developed by IT.
observations.reason: Conduct of physical count of properties was not completed, hence accuracy of the reconciled accounts cannot be verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management hire additional qualified regular employees in rescue operations pursuant to Item 5.6 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council, Department of Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and Civil Service Commission. We recommended further that, in subsequent trainings, Management ensure that personnel nominated to attend trainings are regular employees of the City. Additionally, require the JOs who attended the training to immediately cascade the knowledge and skills they learned from the training.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO no longer allowed JOs attend trainings.
observations.reason: The target implementation date for the creation of new position for regular employees in the CDRRM Office is in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Panglungsod to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operation of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10.
observations.managementAction: Management had started preparing the 5-year Business Plan.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang Panglungsod was requested to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operation of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant issue demand letters to farmer-beneficiaries and submit liquidation report to account for the release/disposal of seedlings and fertilizers and require the City Agriculturist to monitor the use of tractors and other farm equipment and submit a status report on the JPIP.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant sent demand letters to farmer- beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The project was a failure and was later discontinued. Farmers could not afford to pay their loans. Management plans to request the write-off of these accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, particularly the TCTs as an indispensable requirement on the procurement of land in accordance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Management commenced processing for the title to Deniel J. Teves and subdivision plan of Pelicito Trupa are with the Melinda Bolves Registry of Deeds.
observations.reason: Documents for transfer of land properties are with the Registry of Deeds; transfer of title to the City yet to be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management reimburse the LDRRMF for the cost of the BCDRRMC Building and furniture and fixtures from the General Fund. We further recommended that the CDRRMC approve only disaster-related projects, programs and activities properly chargeable to the LDRRMF in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Management had discussed with the DRRM Council and LCE for the appropriate reimbursement.
observations.reason: Management is still requesting supplemental appropriation during the 3rd quarter of 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to make representation with the Provincial Government either to request for the repair/construction of a new spillway/bridge or to get their approval for the City Government to assume its repair or the construction of a new spillway/bridge for productive, developmental, or welfare purposes, in the exercise of their governmental powers and functions pursuant to Sections 18 and 455(4) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Construction of Spillway/Bridge in Shangrila, Barangay Kalumboyan, is now incorporated in the 2022 AIP.
observations.reason: Implementation still in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management decongest the warehouse by delivering construction materials to the project sites and dispose unserviceable materials stocked there and consider immediate expansion of the warehouse to provide adequate storage space for all materials to secure them against unauthorized use and protect them from deteriorating effects of the elements in order to prevent wastage of government resources.
observations.managementAction: Materials for the specific projects were delivered to the satellite offices where the project is located.
observations.reason: Disposal of unserviceable properties is yet to be scheduled for public auction. The public auction was delayed due to COVID19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the PRAISE Committee, in coordination with the City Budget Officer, to determine the monetary savings as a result of the exemplary efforts and superior accomplishments of officials and employees pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01 series of 2001. observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented – The BAC had overlooked the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and the TWG comply strictly with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the conduct of eligibility check and post-qualification procedures to ensure that government contracts are awarded to qualified contractors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Not Implemented – The BAC had overlooked the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the City Accountant review and analyze the A/P amounting to ₱74,602,743.45 as of December 31, 2019 and recommend for reversion of those that remained outstanding for two years or more for which no administrative or judicial claim had been filed in accordance with Section 98 of P.D. We further recommended that the City Accountant effect the necessary corrections/adjustments for the errors in recording and excess obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operation of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Management had started preparing the 5-year Business Plan. Preparation of the Plan takes some time and requires adsitional manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant issue demand letters to farmer-beneficiaries and submit liquidation report to account for the release/disposal of seedlings and fertilizers and require the City Agriculturist to monitor the use of tractors and other farm equipment and submit a status report on the JPIP.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The City Accountant sent demand letters to farmer-benefciaries. Partially Implemented – The project was a failure and was later discontinued. Farmers could not afford to pay their loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management reimburse the LDRRMF for the cost of the BCDRRMC Building and furniture and fixtures from the General Fund. We further recommended that the CDRRMC approve only disaster-related projects, programs and activities properly chargeable to the LDRRMF in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Management had discussed with CDRRM Council and LCE for the appropriate reimbursement. The recommendation will take some time because it will undergo the usual budgeting process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, particularly the TCTs as an indispensable requirement on the procurement of land in accordance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The Management conducted surveys of the properties. Certificate of Titles will be processed after the survey.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the PRAISE Committee, in coordination with the City Budget Officer, to determine the awards monetary savings as a result of the exemplary efforts and superior accomplishments of officials and employees pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01 series of 2001.
observations.managementAction: AAR Exemplary Services Incentive Bonus issue was referred to Management but Management was not able to determine the monetary savings.
observations.reason: Management was not able to determine the monetary savings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of Section 481 of Republic Act No. 7160, the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, COA Circular Nos. 2012-003 and 98-002 on the hiring of legal consultants.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management insisted that the two legal consultants did not perform functions of a legal officer needing clearance from Solicitor General.
observations.reason: Management found it hard to comply with the requirements of the Solicitor General. They insisted that the two legal consultants did not perform functions of a legal officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR Management require that all employees be required to refund overpayments and that henceforth observe the applicable regulations on granting of fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management filed an appeal on April 29, 2015 with COA Regional Office VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended further that the RPCPPE be reconciled with the related property and accounting records to ascertain the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Management conducted 2018 physical inventory, however, it was not completed. Some departments failed to submit their RPCCPE for 2018 because they could not easily locate their properties. CGSO demand from the different departments and other government agencies their respective lists of properties as basis in the conduct of physical count and locate their properties.
observations.reason: Only 50% of the total number of offices submitted their RCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the TCTs as an indispensable requirement on the procurement of land in accordance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 and Management agreed to submit the schedule of the parcels of land. A schedule of the parcels of land was prepared, duly certified by the GSO and Assessor.
observations.reason: survey plan is not yet available. Also, there were problems on the road right of way.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) stop capitalizing interests on loans for non-qualifying assets and completed projects in accordance with PPSAS 5 and (b) effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The MOAs were perfected at a later date. Management assured that there was no MOA as discussed in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management record the transactions only upon perfection of the contractual provisions in order to provide users accurate financial information at a particular reporting date in accordance with IPSAS 29.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 and Management agreed to record the transactions only when the City becomes a party to the contracts. Management assured that transactions will be strictly observed and complied with in the future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and that future activities be charged only to disaster risk management activities related to LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 and Management agreed that the City was not able to utilize the Best KAP 2019 CDRRMF. Management implemented a new program called "Program for Resiliency in Disaster and Emergency Responsiveness" under the supplemental General Fund, not in the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, in subsequent trainings, Management ensure that personnel nominated to attend trainings are regular employees of the City. Additionally, require the JOs who attended the training to immediately cascade the knowledge and skills they learned from the training.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 and Management adhered to Item 5.6 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1. CDRRMO no longer sent JOs to expensive trainings. Those who were able to attend were required to cascade the knowledge and skills.
observations.reason: The opening of relevant new plantilla positions is still pending approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the utilization of the 20% DF. We further recommended that expenditures for ineligible projects shall be refunded by the General Fund Proper. We also recommended that Management re-align, through a Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution, ineligible projects which were not yet implemented.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Management adhered strictly to provisions of DILG and DBM Circular on the use of 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Environmental Officer and the City Planning Development Officer submit to DENR-EMB the complete mandatory requirements for the issuance of the ECCs in accordance with Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1586 dated June 11, 1978.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Management secured the ECC for Narra Water Park (ECC-OL-R07-2019-0349) and Danapa Eco Park (ECC-OL-R07-0232).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the CCMDO and other concerned City officials regularly monitor and supervise the projects engaged by the NGOs/POs to avoid possible loan diversion, poor management and unwillingness to repay loans.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Frequent visits and close monitoring of project status were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revert the balance of the LGSEF to the General Fund-Proper and utilize the same subject to the budgetary requirements and general limitations under Sections 324 and 325, respectively, of R.A. No. 7160, and other existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The LGSEF was reverted to General Fund-Proper.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the provisions of the MOA, particularly on the target date of completion, ensuring that POWs are prepared without delay and complete specifications of materials are clearly indicated in the PR.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Management strictly adhered to the conditions of the MOA and imposed sanctions to contractors for delays incurred in project implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor immediately enter into a MOA with PCSO pursuant to Section 40 of the 2016 Revised IRR for the STL Operations of the PCSO so that the STL share can be utilized for health programs, medical assistance and services, and charities of the City.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The LCE already secured the signatory authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod.
observations.reason: The MOA was still pending due to change of signatory at the PCSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, henceforth, require the CCMDO, General Services Officer and Inspectorate Team to see to it that deliveries of procured goods conform to the specifications in the PO in accordance with Section 114(a) of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Management strictly observed COA Circular 92-386. Deliveries were inspected and found conforming to PO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 17 in the computation for depreciation of the property, plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The City Accountant used the 5% salvage value in computing depreciation expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, hiring of consultants shall be made through competitive public bidding or any other alternative modes of procurement pursuant to Section 10, Article IV and Section 48, Article XVI of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Hiring of consultants were compliant with RA No. 9184. The consultants were engaged in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules on contract of service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operation of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The preparation of 5-Year Business Plans was still on-going for the operation of slaughterhouse and cemetery.
observations.reason: Limited manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 4(1) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and Section 322 of Republic Act No. 7160. We further recommended that concerned officials and employees be required to refund the amount of CNA incentives received.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management adhered to the recommendations; however, Management did not refund because they insisted that they were entitled to receive the CNA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the personnel concerned immediately refund the excess payment of hazard allowances and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the IRR of Republic Act No. 7305 on the grant of hazard pay.
observations.managementAction: AAR The City implemented the correct rate for hazard pay as effective October 2016 as well as persons liable on the excess payment immediately refund the same to the city government and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions on the grant of hazard pay.
observations.reason: No refund yet pending the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant issue demand letters to farmer-beneficiaries to submit liquidation reports to account for the release/disposal of seedlings and fertilizers and require the City Agriculturist to monitor the use of tractors and other farm equipment and submit a status report on the JPIP.
observations.managementAction: AAR Demand letters were already issued by the City Treasurer and beneficiaries were committed to pay. Frequent visits to the premises of the NGOs/POs or a quick follow-up, especially after a missed payment, will allow the City to understand the status of their projects and appropriateness of the loan term and if necessary, to provide assistance in order to control or minimize default/delinquency.
observations.reason: The project did not succeed, thus, it was made difficult to collect from the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track the completion of the details for each real property unit and the information necessary for the establishment of the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and henceforth, the City Treasurer can prepare the certified list of taxpayers to serve as the basis for the City Accountant to take up the receivable and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management fast tracked the completion of the data base. Management already established a database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR the Accounting Office adhere to the provisions of the PAG to IPSAS 17 in the computation of depreciation of the property, plant and equipment.
observations.reason: The Electronic New Government Accounting System (eNGAS) automatically computed the depreciation of the depreciable PPE of the City which uses the 10% of the cost of the salvage value. The Accounting Office cannot amend the system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR Management, direct the City Assessor to fast track the completion of the details for each real property unit and the information necessary for the establishment of the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and henceforth, the City Treasurer can prepare the certified list of taxpayers to serve as the basis for the City Accountant to take up the receivable and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction:
• The new eNGAS roll out does not require setting up of receivables per RPU but only per barangay.
• The breakdown for the RPT/SET will be derived from the records of the City Assessor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the CGSO demand from
the different departments and other government agencies their respective lists
of properties as basis in the conduct of physical count and submit the RPCPPE
for CY 2018 not later than June 30, 2019. We recommended further that the
RPCPPE be reconciled with the related property and accounting records to
ascertain the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts presented in the
financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required
documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012,
particularly the TCTs as an indispensable requirement on the procurement of
land in accordance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992.

Management commented that the Certificate Authorizing Registration (CAR)
for the 17,065 sq. m. lot purchased from Mr. Trupa was already with the Bureau of
Internal Revenue (BIR) and was scheduled to be released on June 25, 2019 per CS
No. 79-0519-008 dated May 16, 2019. Likewise, the CAR for the 164 sq. m. lot was
on process with the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant (a)
stop capitalizing interests on loans for non-qualifying assets and completed
projects in accordance with PPSAS 5 and (b) effect the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to record the transactions
only when the City becomes a party to the contractual provisions of an
instrument in order to provide users accurate financial information at a
particular reporting date in accordance with IPSAS 29.

The City Accountant also commented that she would instruct the employee in
charge to reverse the entry made for NABEFA because the loan was not released
yet.

We recommended that Management require the PRAISE Committee, in
coordination with the City Budget Officer, to determine the monetary savings as
a result of the exemplary efforts and superior accomplishments of officials and
employees pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01 series of 2001.
Monetary awards shall be granted only to individuals or group of individuals
upon determining that their suggestions, inventions, and superior
accomplishments resulted in monetary savings for the LGU.

Management commented that the City Government received various awards
and recognitions as a result of the collaboration and coordination of officials and
employees. Thus, Management deemed it right and proper to reward all employees
with ESIB as monetary incentive for their meritorious performance.

They commented further that DBM Budget Circular No. 2013-3, pursuant to
House of Representatives Joint Resolution (JR) No. 45 and Executive Order No. 80,
directing the adoption of the Performance Based Incentive System (PBIS) for
government personnel includes the Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) and
Performance-Based Bonus (PBB). Since the DBM has not issued the guidelines on
the grant of PBB as a top-up bonus for LGUs, Management granted the ESIB based
on the discretion of the PRAISE Committee.

While we commend Management for the grant to ESIB to all officials and
employees to encourage them to perform well, however, CSC Memorandum Circular
No. 1 is very clear that monetary awards shall be granted only when suggestions,
inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts of individuals or
group of individuals resulted in monetary savings for the LGU. Since the City had
not determined the monetary savings, it should not have granted the ESIB to all
employees and officials.

In addition, the grant of the monetary awards should not be based on the
discretion of the SP because their power to approve budgets and appropriate funds
should always be in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Office (CDRRMO) align its PPAs and limit the
utilization of the LDRRMF to activities related only to disaster risk reduction
management pursuant to Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management hire additional qualified regular
employees in rescue operations pursuant to Item 5.6 of Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council, Department of Interior and Local Government,
Department of Budget and Management and Civil Service Commission.

We recommended further that, in subsequent trainings, Management
ensure that personnel nominated to attend trainings are regular employees of
the City. Additionally, require the JOs who attended the training to
immediately cascade the knowledge and skills they learned from the training.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of
DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017 in the utilization of the 20% DF. We further recommended that
expenditures for ineligible projects shall be refunded by the General Fund
Proper. We also recommended that Management re-align, through a
Sangguniang Panlungsod resolution, ineligible projects which were not yet
implemented.

Management commented that projects such as the Construction of Day Care
Centers, Alternative Learning System Building and the like could be charged to the
20% DF citing RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 on the
implementation of PPAs for the protection, welfare and development of children.
They explained that the Development of Narra Water Park and Danapa Eco-Park
could likewise be charged to the 20% DF because Section 3.2.4 allows capital
expenditures related to the implementation of local economic development where
tourism-related infrastructure can be aptly classified.

It is emphasized that Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the
Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) is the applicable existing
regulations on the use of the 20% DF which supersedes Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2012-120. Therefore, the projects pertaining to the children’s’ welfare should
rather be charged to the 1% IRA for the Local Council for the Protection of Children
(LCPC) or the General Fund. Moreover, tourism-related infrastructure projects while
they partake of capital expenditures, are not among the eligible projects chargeable to
the 20% DF.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Environment &
Natural Resources Officer and the City Planning Development Officer to submit
to DENR-EMB the complete mandatory requirements for issuance of the ECCs
in accordance with Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1586 dated June 11,
1978.

According to the City Planning and Development Coordinator, the application
for ECC was submitted to EMB-NIR (Region 18) in early 2017, but upon the
dissolution of NIR the application was transferred to EMB 7. The applications for the
two eco-parks were denied for lack of necessary documents. He stated, however, that
the LGU had been working on the requirements for submission to the DENR-EMB 7.

We recommended that, henceforth, the CCMDO and other concerned
City officials regularly monitor and supervise the projects engaged by the
NGOs/POs to avoid possible loan diversion, poor management and unwillingness
to repay loans. The frequent visits to the premises of the NGOs/POs or a quick
follow-up, especially after a missed payment, will allow the City to understand
the status of their projects and appropriateness of the loan term (amounts,
frequency of repayments and repayment period) and if necessary, to provide
assistance in order to control or minimize default/delinquency.

The City Treasurer stated in her letter dated May 23, 2019 that she made a
follow-up with the CCMDO for the list of officers and members of the different
NGOs/POs/Borrower so she could send them letters and find out whether the
creditors are still residing in Bayawan City.

We recommended and Management agreed to revert the balance of the
LGSEF to the General Fund-Proper and utilize the same subject to the
budgetary requirements and general limitations under Sections 324 and 325,
respectively, of R.A. No. 7160, and other existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the
provisions of the MOA, particularly on the target date of completion, ensuring
that POWs are prepared without delay and complete specifications of materials
are clearly indicated in the PR.

The City Engineer explained that the NCDC was not completed on time
because of the change in location, a low lying area which needed additional back fill
and concreting materials, thus, the subsequent/revised preparation of the POW was
not done immediately.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Government
immediately enter into a MOA with PCSO pursuant to Section 40 of the 2016
Revised IRR for the STL Operations of the PCSO so that the STL share can be
utilized for health programs, medical assistance and services, and charities of the
City.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, require the CCMDO,
General Services Officer and Inspectorate Team to see to it that deliveries of
procured goods conform to the specifications in the PO in accordance with
Section 114(a) of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

The CCMDO explained that the 12 heads of carabao were accepted and
inspected on August 10, 2018 and August 17, 2018, respectively, with a total
aggregate weight of 2,144 kilos per AIR No. 300-18-08-116. The eight heads of
carabao, on the other hand, were accepted on September 19, 2018 and inspected on
September 20, 2018 with aggregate weight of 1,208 kilos per AIR No. 300-18-09-
127. Thus, the total weight of the 20 heads of carabao was 3,352 kilos in excess of
the 2,600 kilos as agreed upon with the supplier.

Management claimed that the total weight of the carabaos delivered exceeded
the minimum requirement at the time it was accepted and inspected. However, it is
unreliable because they based it only on the Dawis Livestock Market Slips. The
market slips were dated June 26, 28, July 5, 12 and 19, 2018 prior to the acceptance
date, hence, there was no assurance that the same heads of carabao were delivered on
August 10, 2018. There was also no document showing that the livestock received by
the City actually weighed 130 kilos per head.

We recommended that AAR the City Accountant coordinate with the City General Services Officer in identifying and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify these to the proper asset accounts.
observations.managementAction:
• Subsidiary ledgers of all PPEs are electronically maintained in the eNGAS.
• Assets which are “For ID” accounts were already adjusted per JEV No. 2018-05-002566 and 2018-05-002657.
These were actually assets which description in the SL cards did not match with what was actually counted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR the City Accountant prepare and effect the necessary adjusting entries provided in Part II (page 38) of this report.
observations.managementAction:
• Major repairs of properties and equipment normally took time and not a one-time disbursement while the repair is undergoing, the charges are booked in the Construction in Progress to capture all charges and finally to its appropriate asset account like watercraft and construction and heavy equipment when completed.
• Vacuum Truck Decloggers and Mini Dump Trucks recorded as Motor Vehicles were adjusted to Other Transportation Equipment and Construction and Heavy Equipment, respectively, while Fire Truck was adjusted to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment as recommended.
• Bayawan Integrated Business Center was transferred to Hospitals and Health Center account per JEV No. 2018-06-003552.
• The GK Housing Project was transferred to Other Structures account per JEV No. 2018-06-003552.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR the City Accountant make the necessary adjustments to present the correct balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: All assets acquired prior to CY 2002 were depreciated per JEV No. 2018-05-002561; 2018-05-002511 and 2018-05-002529

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, hiring of consultants shall be made through competitive public bidding or any other alternative modes of procurement pursuant to Section 10, Article IV and Section 48, Article XVI of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
• The consultants were hired in accordance with the Civil Service Commission contract of service. Management started implementing the same starting CY 2019.
• Starting CY 2019 all consultants were hired pursuant to Section 10, Article IV and Section 48, Article XVI of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Consultants were already hired in 2018 before the observation was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass an ordinance recognizing continued operation of the LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: • The preparation of the 5-Year Business Plan is still ongoing. The Sangguniang Panlungsod had started crafting the ordinance recognizing continued operation of the LEEs.
observations.reason: Gathering of the necessary data for preparation for the Business Plan required a long time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop appropriating any amount for similar purpose as for expenses chargeable to EME pursuant to Section 325(h) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer appropriated any amount for EME pursuant to Section 325(h) of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that disbursements for EME and other expenditures be supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents, such as list of guest/visitors, menus, and purpose of the meals were already attached to the Disbursement Voucher (DV).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management limit the grant of authority to approve DVs to the City Administrator to approve disbursement vouchers covering regularly recurring administrative expenses only in accordance with Section 344 of R.A 7160.
observations.managementAction: The grant of authority to approve DVs were limited to regularly recurring administrative expenses. Approval is subject to limited amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of Section 481 of Republic Act No. 7160, the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and COA Circular Nos. 2012-003 and 98-002 on the hiring of legal consultants.
observations.managementAction: Management still continued to hire legal consultants.
observations.reason: Management found it hard to comply with the requirements of the Solicitor General.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 4(1) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and Section 322 of Republic No. 7160. We further recommended that concerned officials and employees be required to refund the amount of CNA incentives received.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested comment from DBM and complied with the guidelines.
observations.reason: No refunds yet pending the issuance of Notice of Disallowance from the previous auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR the personnel concerned immediately refund the overpayment of hazard allowances and henceforth strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the IRR of Republic Act No. 7305 on the grant of hazard pay.
observations.managementAction: The City had implemented the correct rate for hazard pay as effective October 2016 based on the new DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2016 dated July 15, 2016.
observations.reason: No refund yet pending the issuance of Notice of Disallowance from the previous auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from charging entertainment expenses to government funds pursuant to Section 343 of RA. 7160. otherwise, said expenses shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: A Memorandum was issued to all departments of the City to comply with the provision of Section 343 of RA. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and City Treasurer refrain from deducting any amount from the salaries of city officials and employees for the payment of their personal loans with institutions not authorized under Section 48 of the General Provisions of the FY 2015 GAA, after the existing loans shall have been fully paid.
observations.managementAction: Personal loans deducted from salaries were only from authorized cooperatives.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management give priority to the construction of a suitable garage and the General Services Officer to facilitate the issuance of red plates for the vehicles and have them marked “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”.
observations.managementAction: Vehicles were marked with "FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY" and provided with government plates. A lot for the garage was also acquired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management requires all employees concerned to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 and to henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management had filed an appeal on April 29, 2015 with COA Regional Office VII.
observations.reason: No decision of the appeal yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management issue demand letters to farmer-beneficiaries and submit liquidation report to account for the release/disposal of the seedlings and fertilizers and require the City Agriculturist to monitor the use of tractors and other farm equipment and submit a status report on the JPIP.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already issued by the City Treasurer and beneficiaries were made to commit to pay from their produce by the City Agriculturist. However, since the program did not prosper, the City is considering to request for write-off of the balance.
observations.reason: The project did not succeed, thus, it was difficult to collect from beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 4(1) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and Section 322 of Republic Act No. 7160. We further recommended that concerned officials and employees be required to refund the amount of CNA incentives received.
observations.managementAction: No refund was made yet.
observations.reason: Awaiting issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management utilize the 20% Economic
Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, henceforth,
adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 76-41 dated July 30, 1976 on
the prohibition against splitting of requisitions, purchase orders, vouchers and
others.

We recommended that management conduct seminars, workshops and
other similar activities within the locality to avoid wastage of government funds.

We recommended that management exercise prudence in the management
of their resources by limiting expenses for advertisements to activities relating only
to the issuance of agency’s guidelines, rules and regulations, conduct of public
bidding and dissemination of important public announcements in accordance with
AO No. 103 and COA Circular No. 12-003 dated October 29. 2012.

We recommended and management agreed to refrain from charging
expenses for entertainment against government funds pursuant to Section 343 of
Republic Act No. 7160 to avoid audit disallowance.

We recommended that, henceforth, management adhere strictly to the
pertinent provisions of Section 481 of Republic Act No. 7160, the 2016 Revised
IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and COA Circular Nos. 2012-003 and 98-002 on the
hiring of legal consultants.

We recommended and management agreed to fully comply with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to promote transparency and
accountability and to secure the right of the people to information on matters of
public concern.
We recommended that management close the deposit accounts with the PNB and transfer the same either to LBP or DBP in accordance with Section 311 of Republic Act No. 7160, DOF D.O. 2016.
observations.managementAction: The PNB account was closed and transferred to the Land Bank of the Philippines-Bayawan Branch on June 14, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City submit a list of employees with special achievements recognized by an outside group, indicating the amount of monetary savings generated by their suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts. Henceforth, indicate in the list the amount of monetary savings generated by their suggestions and superior accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted 2015 to the audit team list of accomplishments and recognitions with certificates earned for the period June to December 2016. However, it did not indicate in the list the amount of monetary savings generated by their suggestions and superior accomplishments. Moreover, some of the achievements are only given by the LGU to selected employees and not by an outside group.
observations.reason: Insufficient supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that personnel concerned who received and as well as persons liable for the excess payment of hazard allowances immediately refund the same to the city government and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the IRR of Republic Act No. 7305 on the grant of hazard pay.
observations.managementAction: The City had implemented the correct rate for hazard pay effective October 2016 based on the new DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2016 dated July 15, 2016. No refund was made yet.
observations.reason: Awaiting issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management utilize the 20% EDF in accordance with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was conducted with all concerned offices regarding the matter to avoid occurrence of the same in the future. They agreed to open a project code reference for major repairs in order for Accounting to distinguish the capitalizable repairs. The Accounting Office adjusted already the items noted in the audit that were erroneously charged to MOOE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from charging entertainment expenses to government funds pursuant to Section 343 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise, said expenses shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. entertainment expense was inappropriately charged to city funds as discussed in detail in Part II of this Report- Audit Observation No. 5.
observations.reason: entertainment expense was inappropriate-ly charged to city funds as discussed in detail in Part II of this Report- Audit Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management increase the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability for a given period, in accordance with the revised schedule of bond premium rates provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer was already increased and submitted a copy to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 so that the City government could still realize income therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The generator costing P131,744.70 was already auctioned, while the disposal of the rest of the items are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and City Treasurer refrain from deducting any amount from the salaries of city officials and employees for the payment of their personal loans with institutions not authorized under Section 48 of the General Provisions of the FY 2015 GAA, after the existing loans shall have been fully paid.
observations.managementAction: The City has existing MOAs with these cooperatives, thus, they still continue the salary deductions for the loan amortizations. However, the LGU no longer enter into new MOA with cooperatives in 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of the inventories and movable properties of the city.
observations.managementAction: The GSO completed the physical count and reconciliation of Supplies and Materials Inventory as of July 21, 2015 and corresponding report was submitted to COA on March 24, 2015. In CY 2016, no physical inventory was conducted for the inventories and movable properties as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (i) management consider revisiting the memoranda of agreement with the grantees and introduce terms and agreements that would ensure the return of the funds to the coffers of the city; (ii) the Cooperative Officer properly monitor the repayment of loans granted and send demand letters every time monthly amortizations are not paid; and (iii) management initiate legal action against those who still refuse to pay after receipt of demand letters.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the CCMDO and CSWD have conferred with the NGOs and were informed that they are willing to pay/settle their obligations to the City. Others have no capacity to pay.
observations.reason: Some NGOs are no longer active while some were dissolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management give priority to the construction of a suitable garage and the General Services Officer to facilitate the issuance of red plates for the vehicles and have them marked "FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY".
observations.managementAction: All vehicles, except Nissan Sentra, donated by GIZ, were issued red plates and marked "FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY".

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: E-NGAS reports are manually converted to PPSAS format to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all employees concerned to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 and to henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.reason: The disallowance is on appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant issue demand letters to farmer-beneficiaries and submit liquidation report to account for the release/disposal of seedlings and to require the City Agriculturist to monitor the use of tractors and other farm equipment and submit a status report on the JPIP.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already issued by the City Treasurer and beneficiaries were made to commit to pay from their produce by the City Agriculturist. Considering, however, that the program did not prosper as there were no markets for the products, the LGU is contemplating on requesting for write off of the receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the City Assessor to fast track the completion of the details for each real property unit and the information necessary for the establishment of the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and henceforth, the City Treasurer can prepare the certified list of taxpayers to serve as the basis for the City Accountant to take up the receivable and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action yet. Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables were not set up since the City of Bayawan implemented the electronic New Government Accounting System (e-NGAS) on May 1, 2005. The setting up of said receivables was held in abeyance pending the outcome of updated version of e-NGAS as advised by COA e-NGAS team.
observations.reason: The setting up of said receivables was held in abeyance pending the outcome of updated version of e-NGAS as advised by COA e-NGAS team.
